headline: Government used emergency ordinance 7 times for non-emergency legislation
context: The federal government invoked emergency ordinance powers seven times between 2023-2025 for legislation that did not meet traditional emergency criteria, bypassing parliamentary debate.

cards.big: Emergency ordinance used 7 times for non-emergency laws — bypassing Parliament each time
cards.sub: Constitutional emergency power becoming routine executive legislative tool

cards.big: Article 150 emergency powers were designed for existential national threats
cards.sub: Using emergency mechanism for routine legislation subverts constitutional design

cards.big: 7 ordinances affected RM18 billion in fiscal commitments without parliamentary scrutiny
cards.sub: Massive spending decisions made through constitutional shortcut

cards.big: Emergency ordinances lapse unless ratified by Parliament within 6 months — 3 were not ratified
cards.sub: Government allowed ordinances to lapse rather than face parliamentary debate

cards.big: When emergency powers become routine, the real emergency is the erosion of parliamentary democracy
Should emergency ordinances require immediate parliamentary ratification within 30 days?
